videos.title: Markets Weigh Next Phase of AI Trade | Open Interest 6/24/2026
videos.description:
Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." The AI trade faces its next big test as Micron takes the earnings spotlight. Meanwhile, South Korea's SK Hynix pursues a landmark US listing to help fund its AI ambitions. BlackRock's Gargi Chaudhuri, Simplify's Paisley Nardini and Hollow Brook's Philip Richter discuss where investors can find opportunity and what risks they're watching. Plus, MNTN CEO Mark Douglas discusses how AI and live sports are fueling a global advertising boom around the World Cup.

Chapters:
00:00:00 - Open Interest
00:01:31 - OpenAI Announces First Custom AI Chip Broadcom
00:08:43 - Trump: Gas Prices Lower than Under Biden
00:14:00 - High Interest | Mamdani Allies Win Key Primaries, Data Centers 
00:15:37 - Meta, Microsoft Lead $850B Data Center Lease Boom
00:24:03 - Gargi Chaudhuri BlackRock | Oil Falls Below $75 as More Ships Cross Hormuz

00:37:15 -  AI Memory Demand & Supply

videos.title: LIVE: Reuters talks to Energy Secretary Wright on US policy, supply outlook
videos.description:
U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright joins the Reuters Global Energy Forum at a pivotal moment for U.S. energy policy, as the administration balances energy security, affordability, and the transition to lower-carbon sources. With rising global demand, geopolitical uncertainty, and continued debate over the pace and scope of decarbonization, the Energy Department is playing a central role in shaping domestic production, infrastructure investment, and international energy engagement.
 
#reutersnext #energy #markets #middleeast #iranwar

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/

videos.title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
videos.description:
Tuesday’s stock-market slide was what one Wall Street strategist called a “chip-wreck.”
The semiconductor companies that have led this year’s stock-market advance with triple-digit percentage gains were suddenly hit with a wave of doubts about the sustainability of the AI-driven rally.
Daniel Ives, Global Head of Technology as Wedbush Securities, discusses the broader tech landscape as investors remain jittery on high-flying tech stocks.
--------

videos.title: ESPN's Linda Cohn Reflects her Legendary SportsCenter Run
videos.description:
ESPN legend and sports broadcasting trailblazer Linda Cohn is stepping away from the anchor desk following a historic career that spanned more than 30 years.

Cohn joins Bloomberg Intelligence Radio to reflect on the journey that made her the longest-tenured SportsCenter anchor in the network's history. From anchoring thousands of shows to covering unforgettable moments in sports, Cohn discusses the passion that fueled her career, her favorite memories over the years, and why helping inspire the next generation of women in broadcasting means so much to her.
--------

videos.title: The U.S. Has 100K Tons of Nuclear Waste. Why Is There Still No Plan? | WSJ Pro Perfected
videos.description:
The United States has more commercial nuclear waste than any other country in the world. But years after a plan to build a permanent repository unraveled, the nation still relies on temporary storage solutions that were never engineered to last forever.

WSJ speaks with former Nuclear Regulatory Commission Chairman Allison Macfarlane, who breaks down why the country fell behind—and what it will take to finally build a long-term home for these radioactive materials. 

Chapters:
0:00 The U.S.’s nuclear waste problem
0:48 Identifying the waste
2:50 Possible solutions
4:27 The U.S.’s failure so far
5:46 The U.S.’s path forward

Pro Perfected
Experts in engineering and design break down a ubiquitous problem, examining how the world is built and what can make it better.

#Nuclear #Finland #WSJ

videos.title: LIVE: WHO briefing on Ebola outbreak situation in eastern Congo
videos.description:
WHO director-general Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and experts brief media on the Ebola outbreak situation in eastern Congo. 

Congo's Ebola outbreak has the largest ‌number of confirmed cases within the first month of any episode of the disease, a senior WHO official told a briefing on June 23, blaming the surge on its quick spread to urban areas. The Bundibugyo outbreak in Congo that has infected over 1,000 people and killed 267 was detected late and experts say the virus had already been circulating for months before it was officially ⁠declared on May 15.

#ebola #outbreak #health #congo #drc #who #un #TedrosAdhanomGhebreyesus #bundibugyo #live #Reuters #News

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/

videos.title: Zero Fed Rate Hikes Baked Into S&P Forecast, Says Wells Fargo's Cronk
videos.description:
Darrell Cronk, Wells Fargo Wealth and Investment Management CIO, says zero interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve are baked into their forecast that says the S&P 500 will reach 7,900 by the end of this year. He says markets are not in a selloff, he says this is a "rotation within equities." He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance."

videos.title: CNBC Takes A First Look At Slate Auto’s $25,000 Modular Cars
videos.description:
CNBC’s Michael Wayland takes a first look at electric vehicle startup Slate Auto’s modular $25,000 pick up trucks. The cars are affordable and basic, with features that buyers can customize. That includes turning the truck into an SUV. The vehicles had more than 150,000 refundable reservations before their release, despite waning consumer demand in EVs in the U.S. The company is about to find out if the truck can disrupt the market.

Reporting by: Michael Wayland
Produced and Camera by: Natalie Rice
Edited by: Darren Geeter
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Slate Auto

videos.title: Why a $1,000 Mahjong Set Can Take 10 Days to Make | WSJ Coveted
videos.description:
Mahjong is experiencing a widespread resurgence in the U.S., driving demand for the game to unprecedented heights. Halfway across the world, however, the painstaking craft of hand-carving mahjong tiles is quietly fading. Collectors praise hand-carved mahjong sets for their uniqueness and beauty. But the rise of cheaper mass-produced sets threatens the intricate craft.

In Hong Kong, where this artisanship is deeply rooted, the city's last remaining masters are trying to keep the dying tradition alive. WSJ explored how these sets that can go for $1,000 are handcrafted.

Chapters:
0:00The art of creating mahjong tiles
1:28 How to play mahjong
2:45 The history of mahjong and the design
3:37 How to hand carve tiles
5:45 Hand-carved tiles vs. machine-made tiles
7:48 The American mahjong obsession
9:41 Preserving the craft 

WSJ's Coveted highlights the precision and process behind expertly-crafted cult favorites — from the pencils beloved by Disney animators to the sushi knives breaking thousands of years of Japanese tradition. These stories show how the smallest everyday items can be transformed into the ultimate luxury splurge.

#Mahjong #Craftsmanship #WSJ

videos.title: Inside Africa’s Largest Clean Cooking Factory | BURN in Kenya
videos.description:
What does the clean energy transition look like in practice? Join Aki Nishio, World Bank Vice President for Development Finance, on a visit to BURN’s factory in Kenya to see how clean cooking innovation is being scaled across Africa.

BURN is the world’s largest clean cooking company and a leading manufacturer of clean cookstoves, electric cookers, and energy-efficient cooking equipment in Africa. In this video, see how BURN is expanding production across Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Tanzania, Malawi, and beyond—while helping to reduce indoor air pollution, improve health outcomes, and support economic growth.

Learn how IDA financing helped accelerate BURN’s growth, enabling the company to expand production capacity, strengthen manufacturing, and increase access to clean cooking solutions for households, schools, and public institutions—creating jobs and improving lives across the region.

0:00-0:24: What is BURN?
0:25-0:56: Innovation fueled by IDA
0:57-1:44: BURN's Impact
1:45-2:52: The process and testimonial
2:53-4:24: The potential for manufacturing
4:25-5:06: How this creates jobs

What do you think is the biggest opportunity for scaling clean cooking in Africa—finance, manufacturing, consumer access, or policy support?
#CleanCooking #AfricaManufacturing #Kenya #EnergyAccess #DevelopmentFinance

🌐 ABOUT THE WORLD BANK GROUP 
The World Bank GROUP is one of the world’s largest sources of funding and knowledge for low-income countries. Its five institutions share a commitment to reducing poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development on a livable planet. http://www.worldbank.org

videos.title: Why the U.K. Can’t Keep a Prime Minister | WSJ
videos.description:
The U.K. is on the cusp of having its sixth leader in just seven years after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. It comes after Starmer – a former human rights lawyer who returned the Labour Party to power for the first time in 14 years – failed to enact change fast enough for a weary British public and faced growing rebellion from his own lawmakers.

WSJ explains what went wrong in Starmer’s premiership and what comes next for the U.K.

Chapters:
0:00 Starmer resigns
1:02 Starmer’s missteps
3:24 Policy u-turns
4:04 Relationship with Trump
5:01 Local elections
6:29 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers 
break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Starmer #Trump #WSJ

videos.title: How Ukraine and Russia Treat Their Prisoners of War Differently | WSJ
videos.description:
Chernihiv, Ukraine — After almost two years in a Russian prison, Ukrainian soldier Oleksandr “Sasha” Kenev was exchanged in a prisoner swap. As he stepped off the bus onto home soil, his wife Khrystyna was there to meet him. What followed were weeks of rehab and therapy as Sasha tried to readjust to life back home in Ukraine. 

WSJ follows Sasha’s journey, and takes a look at what life is like for those who are still waiting for their loved ones to be exchanged. 

Chapters:
0:00 Russia and Ukraine prisoner swaps
1:08 A Ukrainian soldier returns home
4:45 Sasha’s backstory and arrest
7:15 Conditions in Russian captivity
8:54 Rehab and recovery after returning home
11:19 Inside a Russian POW camp in Ukraine 
13:55 Russia vs. Ukraine prisoners of war
15:32 Adapting to life after returning home

WSJ Originals features in-depth reporting, investigations and on-the-ground journalism that uncovers the forces shaping business, politics and the world around us.

#Ukraine #Russia #WSJ

videos.title: How Can Water Solutions Feed 10 Billion People on a Livable Planet?
videos.description:
How can we feed a global population of 10 billion by 2050? This video explores how smarter water management can transform food systems. Drawing on the Nourish and Flourish report, it shows how aligning food production with water availability can boost yields, create jobs, improve livelihoods, and protect the environment. videos.title: How Snack Companies Could Lose Millions Of Dollars Over ‘MAHA’ Laws
videos.description:
The MAHA or Make America Healthy Again movement, inspired by U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is changing SNAP benefits nationwide. It's also influenced more than 20 states to ban junk food made with synthetic dyes and additives from K-12 schools. CNBC’s Brandon Gomez has more on what this will mean for snack companies and SNAP users.

Reporting by: Brandon Gomez
Produced and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park, Christina Locopo
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images, Gov. Kim Reynolds

videos.title: What The U.S.-Iran Deal Could Mean For Markets And Your Wallet
videos.description:
President Trump and Iranian leaders signed a memorandum of understanding that should reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lower oil prices and ease shipping costs. The deal could bring relief for consumers through cheaper gas, airfare and goods, but risks around regional security, inflation, Fed policy and interest rates remain.

Chapters:
0:00 Introduction
0:38 Interest rates
4:23 What's next?

Produced by: Charlotte Morabito
Reporters: Pippa Stevens, Dan Murphy
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage: Getty Images

videos.title: How This Hospitality Mogul Turns Relationships Into Millions | The WSJ Money Interview
videos.description:
David Grutman, the founder of Groot Hospitality, is constantly hunting for opportunities to forge relationships—and collaborations—among people he meets. The approach has helped him expand from hot spots like the Miami club LIV to media, including producing an upcoming Netflix series starring Alix Earle, and a growing portfolio of venture bets. Kim Kardashian, whom he met on the Miami nightlife scene around two decades ago, wrote the foreword to his book, “Take It Personal.” He says he chats with more than 200 people a day and shared what he’s learned about building businesses along the way with us in The WSJ Money Interview.

Watch the full episode here: https://on.wsj.com/4fTIRFQ 

Chapters:
0:00 Meet David Grutman
0:30 The common thread across Grutman’s properties
1:35 LIV nightclub
2:19 Going sober as a nightclub owner
3:14 Getting into the restaurant business
5:30 Building his own brands and the power of IP
6:26 Selling to Live Nation and building wealth
7:40 Investing in other companies and collaborating
8:23 Production company and Alix Earle show

The WSJ Money Interview
What can some of the world’s most interesting tycoons teach us about money and success? WSJ’s Gunjan Banerji sits down with highfliers for a candid conversation about how they made it. Have an idea for a future episode? Or a topic you’d love our next guest to weigh in on? Write to the host at gunjan.banerji@wsj.com

#Business #Money #WSJ

videos.title: Why Abercrombie Is Copying Aritzia's Playbook
videos.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch's torrid growth has started to slow. To counteract moderating sales and increasing competition, the retailer is selling third-party shoe brands like Puma, Frye, Hunter and GH Bass. CNBC's Gabrielle Fonrouge reports.

Reporter: Gabrielle Fonrouge
Produced, Shot & Edited by: Tasia Jensen
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi

videos.title: Where Markets Think Fed Chair Warsh Is Taking Interest Rates
videos.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s management of assets, inflation and public communications. Markets are pricing in higher odds for future rate hikes, rather than rate cuts as emphasized by Chair Warsh in confirmation hearings. CNBC’s Matt Peterson, Steve Liesman and Jeff Cox report.

Reporters: Steve Liesman, Jeff Cox and Matt Peterson
Produced and Edited by: Carlos Waters
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

videos.title: Daron Acemoglu on the Good-Jobs Imperative — ABCDE 2026
videos.description:
The 2026 Annual Bank Conference on Development Economics (ABCDE) opens with a keynote on on the urgent need for good jobs across the world, featuring World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill and Nobel laureate Daron Acemoglu.

Over the next 25 years, countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and the Middle East will see an unprecedented surge in their working-age population. This shift could become one of the defining economic opportunities of the century—or a source of growing instability.

World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill will set the stage for this important discussion, outlining the challenges and opportunities the world will face in creating productive and rewarding jobs for the next generation.

- Learn more: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/daron-acemoglu-on-the-good-jobs-imperative-abcde-2026

ABOUT US 🌐 The World Bank Group (WBG) is one of the world’s largest sources of funding and knowledge for low-income countries. Its five institutions share a commitment to reducing poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development on a livable planet. http://www.worldbank.org

videos.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
videos.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

Chapters:
0:00-54:00 Introduction
00:55-03:16 Chapter 1: Computing Costs
03:17-05:27 Chapter 2: Demand
05:28-07:21 Chapter 3: Setting a Benchmark

Edited by: Andrea Miller

videos.title: The Next Phase Of The Obesity Drug Race
videos.description:
Five years after Wegovy’s FDA approval, the obesity drug market has evolved rapidly. Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly introduced GLP-1 pills this year and others like Structure Therapeutics and Zealand Pharma are trying to fight for market share.  But the space is getting crowded. CNBC’s Angelica Peebles has the story from the American Diabetic Association’s conference in New Orleans.

Chapters:
00:00 - 00:37 Introduction
00:38 - 03:10 Mounting competition 
03:11 - 5:38 Next steps in innovation 

Reporting by: Angelica Peebles
Senior Field Producer: Leanne Miller
Edited by: Darren Geeter
Animation by: Jason Reginato, Emily Park

videos.title: World Bank Group Youth Summit Pitch Competition 2026 – Finals
videos.description:
Join us for the Pitch Competition Finals, where selected young entrepreneurs present their ideas at the World Bank Group Youth Summit. The competition brings together participants aged 18–35 from around the world, working on solutions to development challenges, including job creation, education and skills, and entrepreneurship.

During this session, finalists will pitch their projects and share how their ideas can move from concept to implementation, with a focus on real-world impact.

↘️ Learn more about the six projects in competition:

• Crustea Indonesia, the company behind Eco-Aerator, is advancing sustainable aquaculture through an AI- and IoT-enabled aeration system that helps shrimp farmers monitor water quality in real time, automate oxygen control, reduce energy costs, and improve productivity. [Winner of the Early Ventures Award]

• Bionic Soul is making mobility more accessible by developing high-quality and more affordable lower-limb prosthetics for underserved communities, with a focus on the MENA region and Africa.

• eAgro Pvt Ltd is bringing climate-smart agricultural intelligence to smallholder farmers through AI advisory tools that work even without reliable internet, helping farmers improve yields, reduce climate risks, and increase income.

• PanFa Technologies is using satellite data and machine learning to scale environmentally friendly rice cultivation, helping farmers reduce methane emissions, save water, and unlock carbon-credit revenue. [Winner of the Ideas Award]

• SankofaFresh is building an AI-powered cold chain platform for smallholder farmers in Ghana, combining affordable cold storage, digital market access, and smart logistics to reduce post-harvest losses and improve farmer livelihoods. [Winner of the Audience Choice Award]

• Lesto Circular is turning idle equipment and materials into jobs and circular opportunities by digitizing underused assets and connecting them with entrepreneurs and SMEs that need affordable tools to grow.

Learn more: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/world-bank-group-youth-summit
#WBGYouthSummit

-Opening sessions with Ajay Banga and Makhtar Diop: https://youtube.com/live/CHY6s3WFcSE ​‌

videos.title: Managing Director & Chief Knowledge Officer Paschal Donohoe | 2026 Youth Summit
videos.description:
In this fireside chat, the World Bank Group Managing Director & Chief Knowledge Officer Paschal Donohoe reflects on what it means to lead a “Knowledge Bank” and how data, evidence, and global expertise shape better outcomes. He will explore how strong knowledge systems can help ensure young people have access to meaningful and sustainable job opportunities.

The Youth Summit is the World Bank Group's largest annual event for youth (ages 18-35) worldwide. It will be held on June 11-12, 2026, featuring activities and engagements both at the World Bank Group headquarters in Washington DC and across various regions. Under the theme #FutureWorks: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age, the 2026 edition of the Summit will center on education and skills, entrepreneurship, and agriculture.

Learn more: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/world-bank-group-youth-summit
#WBGYouthSummit

videos.title: Job Creation in Fragile Settings | Talking Development
videos.description:
In this episode of Talking Development, World Bank Managing Director Anna Bjerde speaks with Basima Abdulrahman, Founder and CEO of KESK in Iraq, and Brian Kelly, Founder and CEO of Anzana Electric Group, about what it takes to build businesses and create jobs in countries affected by fragility, conflict and violence. From solar energy in Iraq to electricity projects across Africa, they discuss how skills, local ecosystems, and private investment can help turn uncertainty into opportunity.

00:00 Why jobs matter in fragile settings
01:13 Building a business in post-conflict Iraq
03:03 Power, investment, and scaling in Africa
05:13 How businesses create jobs directly and indirectly
10:57 What governments can do to support investment



ABOUT THE WORLD BANK 🌐 The World Bank is one of the world’s largest sources of funding and knowledge for low-income countries. Its five institutions share a commitment to reducing poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development on a livable planet. http://www.worldbank.org

videos.title: Jobs in a Changing World: What’s Real & What’s Next | Youth Summit 2026
videos.description:
The World Bank Group Youth Summit returns for its 13th edition, bringing together young people (ages 18–35) from around the world to explore the most pressing issues shaping their future. Under the theme #FutureWorks: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age, the 2026 Summit will focus on education and skills, entrepreneurship, and agriculture.

The mission of the Summit is to:
-Empower youth to explore innovative solutions to development challenges
-Equip youth with the tools to create and participate in impactful projects
-Foster dialogue between youth, the World Bank Group, and other key global stakeholders
-Find out more about the full Youth Summit program and activities.

👤SPEAKERS & AGENDA

videos.title: IDA in Kenya: Supporting Health Across Generations
videos.description:
IDA investments are helping strengthen healthcare systems across Kenya—from maternal and newborn care to disease prevention and emergency preparedness. Featuring reflections from Aki Nishio, alongside the voices of healthcare workers, patients, and families, this video highlights how IDA support is improving lives and building healthier futures across generations.
Learn more: https://ida.worldbank.org/en/country/kenya?cid=ECR_YT_Worldbank_EN_EXT

videos.title: El desarrollo en contextos de FCV en un orden internacional cambiante | Foro sobre Fragilidad 2026
videos.description:
Esta sesión plenaria de alto nivel se transmitirá públicamente en el sitio web del Banco Mundial y reunirá a funcionarios gubernamentales, profesionales del desarrollo y socios para conversar sobre cómo lograr un impacto duradero en contextos frágiles y afectados por conflictos.

videos.title: High-Level Plenary: Development in FCV in a Shifting International Order | Fragility Forum 2026
videos.description:
The World Bank Group Fragility Forum 2026 (June 8-10, 2026) brought together global leaders to align on priorities in an increasingly complex fragility, conflict, and violence (FCV) landscape.

This high-level plenary session was streamed publicly on WB Live, bringing together government officials, development practitioners, and partners to discuss how to deliver lasting impact in fragile and conflict-affected settings.

👤SPEAKERS 
- Mohamed Juldeh Jalloh, Vice President, Republic of Sierra Leone
- Haneen Sayed, Minister of Social Affairs, Lebanon
- Alexandar De Croo, Administrator, @UNDP 
- Birama B. Sidibé, Founder, Agro Biotech
- Ndiame Diop, Vice President for Eastern and Southern Africa, World Bank Group

01:52 Makhtar Diop on Private Sector Investment and Job Creation in FCV Contexts
12:42 Development in a Shifting International Order
25:53 Lebanon's Recovery Challenges and Displacement Crisis
33:02 Global Fragility and Emerging Geopolitical Risks
40:14 The Future of Foreign Aid and Development Finance
47:49 Private Sector Perspectives on Fragility and Growth
53:57 Economic Opportunities Across Fragile African States
01:08:10 Mission 300: Energy Access as a Driver of Development
01:14:34 Long-Term Partnerships in Fragile Contexts
01:23:24 Mobilizing Partnerships and Financing for Impact
01:35:33 Closing Reflections: Youth, Opportunity, and Reasons for Optimism

Watch the public sessions on World Bank Live: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/fragility-forum-2026?cid=ECR_YT_Worldbank_EN_EXT

videos.title: Le développement dans les états fragiles face à un ordre international en mutation | Fragility Forum
videos.description:
Cette session plénière de haut niveau sera diffusée publiquement sur WB Live, réunissant des responsables gouvernementaux, des praticiens du développement et des partenaires pour discuter de la manière d’obtenir un impact durable dans les contextes fragiles et touchés par les conflits.

👤INTERVENANTS & PROGRAMME (toutes les heures sont en heure de la côte Est des États-Unis)
Allocution d’ouverture par Makhtar Diop, Directeur général, IFC
Table ronde : Le développement dans un ordre international en mutation
- Mohamed Juldeh Jalloh, Vice-président, République de Sierra Leone
- Haneen Sayed, Ministre des Affaires sociales, Liban
- Alexandar De Croo, Administrateur, PNUD
- Birama B. Sidibé, Fondateur, Agro Biotech
- Ndiame Diop, Vice-président pour l’Afrique de l’Est et australe, Groupe de la Banque mondiale

À PROPOS DE LA BANQUE MONDIALE 🌐 Le Groupe de la Banque mondiale est l'une des principales sources mondiales de financement et de partage de connaissances au service des pays à revenu faible et intermédiaire. Il regroupe cinq institutions qui poursuivent un objectif commun : réduire la pauvreté, accroître une prospérité partagée et promouvoir un développement durable.
